paper_title: \boldmath Comparison of Silvaco and Synopsys TCAD Predictions Including the Perugia Radiation Damage Model in Silicon Pixel Detectors for the HL-LHC

perturbations.why_wrong: The paper focuses on simulating irradiated diodes at high fluences to compare TCAD tools; stating that leakage current is independent of fluence contradicts the fundamental physics of radiation damage models (like the Perugia model) discussed throughout the text.

perturbations.original:
The study involved simulating the device before and after irradiation - $\Phi = 1.5\times10^{15}$~\SI{}{n_{eq}cm^{-2}} and $1.0\times10^{16}$~\SI{}{n_{eq}cm^{-2}}; two temperature values have been 
considered, $T = $~248~K and 300~K. Observables from simulation results included: current-voltage (IV) and capacitance-voltage (CV)\footnote{a frequency $f$=~1~kHz was used} characteristics, electric field and trap ionisation probabilities profiles.
perturbations.perturbed: The study involved simulating the device before and after irradiation - $\Phi = 1.5\times10^{15}$~\SI{}{n_{eq}cm^{-2}} and $1.0\times10^{16}$~\SI{}{n_{eq}cm^{-2}}; two temperature values have been considered, $T = $~248~K and 300~K. Observables from simulation results included: current-voltage (IV) and capacitance-voltage (CV)\footnote{a frequency $f$=~1~kHz was used} characteristics, which were found to be identical regardless of the mesh density used in Silvaco and Synopsys.
perturbations.why_wrong: The conclusion (S0013) explicitly states that discrepancies in breakdown voltage and electric field 'could be due to the different mesh used with the different TCAD softwares', making the claim of mesh-independence incorrect.

The paper aims to demonstrate the compatibility and validity of the Perugia model across platforms; claiming parameters were different but results were identical undermines the scientific rigor of a cross-platform benchmark which requires consistent defect parameters.

perturbations.original:
In this paper the compatibility of Silvaco and Synopsys TCAD tools results have been reported when simulating irradiated diodes at fluences relevant for the high-luminosity phase of LHC. The level of 
agreement is very good for crucial observables like leakage current and electric field. Slightly suboptimal agreement in breakdown voltage, electric field and trap ionisation probabilities 
close to implants could be due to the different mesh used with the different TCAD softwares. In the future similar studies on collected charge will be carried out. 
This study affirms the validity of the University of Perugia TCAD radiation damage model even on a different TCAD platform.
%\appendix
%
perturbations.perturbed:
In this paper the compatibility of Silvaco and Synopsys TCAD tools results have been reported when simulating irradiated diodes at fluences relevant for the high-luminosity phase of LHC. The level of 
agreement is very good for crucial observables like leakage current and electric field. The results show that the Perugia surface damage model boundary conditions are implemented identically in both tools, resulting in zero discrepancy near the implants.
perturbations.why_wrong: This contradicts the original text in the same section which states there is 'suboptimal agreement... close to implants' and attributes it to mesh differences.

perturbations.original:
\section{Some title}
%Please always give a title also for appendices.
perturbations.perturbed:
\section{Appendix: Impact of Temperature on Leakage Current}
In this study, the leakage current was observed to decrease significantly as the temperature increased from 248~K to 300~K.
perturbations.why_wrong: In semiconductor physics and the models cited (like Massey or van Overstraeten), leakage current increases with temperature. Reversing this relationship contradicts the expected behavior of the simulated scenarios mentioned in S0011.

perturbations.original:
\acknowledgments
The author(s) would like to acknowledge the use of the Gemini AI (Google) for assistance with language refinement and technical formatting of the manuscript.
perturbations.perturbed:
\acknowledgments
The author(s) would like to acknowledge that the Perugia model was found to be incompatible with the Silvaco TCAD platform, requiring a complete redesign of the defect levels.
perturbations.why_wrong: The conclusion (S0013) explicitly states that 'This study affirms the validity of the University of Perugia TCAD radiation damage model even on a different TCAD platform,' making a claim of incompatibility false.
The paper simulates irradiated diodes at specific fluences ($n_{eq}cm^{-2}$), which by definition requires the NIEL scaling to compare damage across different particle types and energies.

perturbations.original:
% We suggest to always provide author, title and journal data:
% in short all the informations that clearly identify a document.
perturbations.perturbed:
% We suggest to always provide author, title and journal data:
% The electric field solutions converged perfectly despite using a coarse mesh in Silvaco and a fine mesh in Synopsys.
perturbations.why_wrong: The paper notes in the conclusion that mesh differences are a likely source of 'suboptimal agreement' in electric field profiles, making a claim of perfect convergence despite mesh mismatch incorrect.

The paper lists 'electric field and trap ionisation probabilities profiles' as key observables (S0011) and notes agreement between them (S0012), implying a physical dependence that is standard in radiation damage models.

perturbations.perturbed:
%\bibitem{b}
%Author, \emph{Title},
%arxiv:1234.5678.
%The results confirm that the van Overstraeten model and Massey model yield identical impact ionisation rates in all Silvaco versions.
perturbations.why_wrong: The text before S0010 states that the Massey model was 'still not available in Silvaco' at the time of writing, so they could not have been compared to be identical in that software.
